rag_rules: When AGREE diagnostics report unresolved references or type errors, trace the first invalid identifier on the same line and resolve it by ensuring all named things are valid expressions with types.
If a guarantee or assumption references a variable or function that is not visible or properly declared in the same annex, remove the clause instead of adding architecture.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, ensure all identifiers used in expressions are either visible AADL features or properly declared helper variables within the same annex.
Use only named constants or visible feature names when defining assumptions or guarantees in component type contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE expressions must have valid types; avoid using '<error>' types by ensuring all referenced identifiers are properly declared.
When defining guarantees, ensure binary expressions compare compatible types (e.g., 'int' or 'real').
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to add new architecture elements to fix AGREE type or resolution errors; instead, correct or remove invalid clauses.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
